---
title: "NFL Models"
---

Model cards for the SportsDataverse / nflverse NFL models. Each card reads like a
methodology write-up — descended from the **nflfastR EP/WP/CP post** and the
**nflverse `fastrmodels` / `nfl4th`** model recipes — and flows
**Overview → Model features → The model → Calibration / Parity → Limitations →
Provenance**.

These are faithful re-implementations of the nflfastR / nfl4th models, retrained
on the **full 1999–2025 history** and bundled into `sportsdataverse`. They are
held to **two** evidentiary standards:

1. **nflfastR parity** *(the headline)* — every play is scored by both this model
   and nflverse's own published artifact, and the columns are correlated on the
   model domain (kickoffs/PATs feature-substituted as nflfastR does). See
   [Parity](parity.md).
2. **Leave-one-season-out (LOSO) calibration** — retrain per held-out season,
   pool the out-of-fold predictions, and plot the nflfastR signature calibration
   curves (each card's *Calibration* section).

## Core play-level models

| Model | Predicts | nflfastR parity |
|---|---|---|
| [Expected Points](ep.md) | next-score EP (7-class softprob) | `ep` **r 0.996** · `epa` **0.994** |
| [Win Probability — spread](wp_spread.md) | in-game WP with the Vegas line | `vegas_wp` **r 0.998** |
| [Win Probability — naive](wp_naive.md) | spread-free in-game WP | `wp` **r 0.997** |
| [CPOE](cpoe.md) | completion % over expected | `cpoe` scale-correct (pp) |
| [Expected YAC](xyac.md) | yards-after-catch distribution (76-class) | faithful `add_xyac` port |

## Fourth-down decision suite

The go / punt / field-goal / two-point recommendation (the nfl4th surface) is
built from five components, each scored through the win-probability surface:

| Model | Predicts | parity |
|---|---|---|
| [Fourth-Down Yards](fourth_down.md) | go-for-it gain distribution (76-class) | mean-gain **r 0.9974** |
| [Field Goal](fg.md) | placekick make probability | attempted-cells **r 0.980** |
| [Two-Point](two_pt.md) | two-point success probability | P(success) **r 0.872** (vintage-drift ceiling) |
| [Punt distribution](punt.md) | post-punt field-position distribution | freq-weighted TV **0.065** |
| [Decision WP](nfl4th_wp.md) | home-perspective WP for the EV comparison | P(win) **r 0.9947** |

## Tendency & quarterback efficiency

| Model / metric | Predicts | parity |
|---|---|---|
| [Expected Pass](xpass.md) | dropback probability (`pass_oe`) | P(pass) **r 0.9957** |
| [QBR](qbr.md) | ESPN-style 0–100 QB rating from EPA components | EPA-explainable reconstruction |
| [dakota](dakota.md) | QB efficiency (EPA + CPOE composite) | derived metric (not a model) |

The `wpa` first-difference correlates ≈0.89 against nflverse — an SNR ceiling, not
a bug (the derivation is exact when fed nflverse's own `wp`; the residual is
per-play WP-model noise amplified by first-differencing). See [Parity](parity.md).

## Regenerating

Calibration figures + metrics are regenerated by the NFL model-report tool
(leave-one-season-out → calibration tables, figures, `metrics.json`); the site
then rebuilds with `quarto render`. Models retrain from the nflfastR-parity PBP;
the parity gate re-runs the column correlations above.
